data.title: Stock index futures inch up as core CPI comes in slightly cooler
data.description:
Stock index futures were slightly higher on Friday as the core retail inflation figures for March came in slightly cooler than expected. 

Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) rose 0.3%, S&P 500 futures (SPX) edged up 0.3%, and Dow Jones Industrial Average futures (INDU) advanced 0.2%. 

Treasury yields moved higher across the curve. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) climbed 2 basis points to 4.30%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) increased 2.8 basis points to 3.80%. The 30-year yield (US30Y) advanced 1.4 basis points to 4.90%.

March U.S. core Consumer Price Index (excludes food and energy): +0.2% M/M vs. +0.3% consensus and +0.2% and +2.6% Y/Y vs. +2.7% consensus and +2.5% prior.

Headline CPI, including food and energy prices: +0.9% M/M vs. +0.9% consensus and +0.3% in February and +3.3% Y/Y vs. +3.4% consensus and +2.4% prior.

Traders will also watch factory orders and preliminary consumer sentiment for April, which is projected at 52.0, below the previous reading.

The uncertainty surrounding a potential truce between the U.S., Israel, and Iran remained at the forefront for traders.

Top gainers in premarket trading included J.B. Hunt Transport Services (JBHT) +4.61%, Insulet (PODD) +2.00%, and International Flavors & Fragrances (IFF) +1.97%. Decliners included Mettler-Toledo International (MTD) -9.84%, DaVita (DVA) -4.91%, and Danaher (DHR) -2.07%.

data.title: Snap teams up with Qualcomm to power smart eyewear unit's products
data.description:
Qualcomm (QCOM) and Snap's (SNAP) new augmented reality eyewear unit, Specs, entered a multi-year agreement to power future generations of Specs with Snapdragon system-on-chip, or SoC.

The companies said this is the first flagship collaboration for Specs, which is launching Specs — an advanced eyewear that integrates digital experiences into the physical world — for consumers later this year.

Specs are standalone, see-through glasses that enable users to see, hear, and interact with digital content just like it’s in the physical space, according to the companies. Specs also provides Lens Studio, a suite of advanced developer tools that powers augmented reality experiences across Specs, Snapchat, and other services.

The companies noted that Specs are powered by Snapdragon XR platforms. By combining edge AI and high performance, low-power compute, Snapdragon platforms provide the foundation that enables intelligent, context‑aware experiences to run directly on device, for faster and more private interactions, the companies added.

"Our work on future generations of Specs will enable power-efficient interactive AR devices that deliver agentic experiences that feel natural, intuitive and integrate seamlessly into daily life," said Qualcomm's President and CEO Cristiano Amon.

The agreement builds on the companies' previous collaborations, as Snapdragon platforms have powered multiple previous generations of Snap’s Spectacles.

data.title: Bitcoin seen topping $75K in April, Kalshi bets shift higher
data.description:
Rising bets on higher April highs for Bitcoin (BTC-USD) were showing up on prediction platform Kalshi, with traders increasingly positioning for a move toward the upper end of the recent range.

Bitcoin (BTC-USD) kickstarted the new quarter with a renewed sense of optimism, ending a grueling five-month losing streak that was the asset’s longest since 2018. 

The world's largest digital currency has had a topsy-turvy start to the year due to a broader risk-off phase. It had fallen as low as $60K in February. 

Kalshi data indicates a 63% probability that Bitcoin trades above $75K in April, up 9 percentage points. Odds of a move above $77.5K stand at 41%, a sharp increase of 33 points, suggesting growing momentum in bullish positioning.

Beyond that, probabilities taper off. The market assigns a 22% chance of Bitcoin exceeding $80K, while the likelihood falls to 14% for a move above $82.5K. More aggressive upside targets remain unlikely, with just a 7% probability of crossing $85K and 4% for $87.5K.

The distribution points to a market that sees moderate upside as plausible but remains cautious on a breakout to new highs within the month. The steep drop-off beyond $80,000 suggests traders view that level as a key hurdle in the near term.

At the time of writing, Bitcoin was up 0.4% to $72K.

data.title: Core CPI comes in slightly cooler than expected in March; headline CPI roughly in line
data.description:
March U.S. core Consumer Price Index (excludes food and energy): +0.2% M/M vs. +0.3% consensus and +0.2% prior, according to data released by the Bureau of Labor Statistics on Friday.

+2.6% Y/Y vs. +2.7% consensus and +2.5% prior.

Headline CPI, including food and energy prices: +0.9% M/M vs. +0.9% consensus and +0.3% in February.

+3.3% Y/Y vs. +3.4% consensus and +2.4% prior.

Developing… Check back for updates.

data.title: Warsh confirmation odds slip after hearing delay, Kalshi shows
data.description:
A delay in the confirmation hearing for Kevin Warsh as Federal Reserve chair candidate is pushing back market expectations for when the process will conclude, according to pricing on prediction platform Kalshi.

Warsh had been scheduled to appear before the Senate Banking Committee on April 16, but the hearing has been postponed, CNBC reported, citing a person familiar with the matter. The hearing is still expected to take place, though no new date has been announced.

Kalshi data shows traders sharply discounting the chances of a near-term confirmation. Odds of Warsh being confirmed before May 1 are just 3%. The probability rises to 48% before May 15 and 64% by June 1.

Further out, markets are more confident. Kalshi pricing implies an 88% chance of confirmation before July 1, with similar odds of 88% for before August 1.

The distribution suggests expectations have shifted toward a late second-quarter timeline. The steep jump between early May and early June indicates the delay has pushed consensus timing by several weeks.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Organon rises as Sun Pharma said to near $12B buyout deal
data.description:
Organon (OGN) shares continued to climb on Friday, adding ~11% in the premarket after media reports from India indicated that Mumbai-based generic drugmaker Sun Pharmaceutical Industries had submitted a $12B binding offer for the U.S. company.

According to the reports, Sun Pharma has reached the final stages of the bid after completing over three months of due diligence. The company is finalizing financing with the support of global banking giants, including JPMorgan (JPM) and MUFG, the reports added.

A potential acquisition would make the transaction the largest overseas M&A deal by an Indian pharmaceutical company. Sun Pharmaceutical shares fell 3% in Mumbai in reaction to the reports.

Shares of the Merck (MRK) spinoff gained in January after India’s Economic Times reported that Sun Pharmaceutical had made a non-binding offer for an all-cash buyout deal with the New Jersey-based company. At the time, the Indian drugmaker was set to begin the due diligence process, after which a binding offer was expected.

data.title: Invesco reports $2,159.5B AUM as of March end
data.description:
• Invesco (IVZ) on Friday announced the preliminary month-end assets under management (AUM) of $2,159.5B, a decrease of 4.4% versus the previous month-end.
  • The firm delivered net long-term inflows of $0.3B in the month.
  • Money market net outflows were $1.8B. AUM was negatively impacted by unfavorable market returns, which decreased AUM by $91 billion. FX movements in the month reduced AUM by $7.0 billion, which was partially offset by reinvested distributions of $0.9 billion.
  • Preliminary average total AUM for the quarter through March 31 was $2,218.9B, and preliminary average active AUM for the quarter through March 31 was $1,146.2B.
  • IVZ -0.0% premarket to $23.57.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Kraken's Fed payment account raises concerns about risks - report
data.description:
While crypto exchange Kraken's (KRAKEN) Federal Reserve master account comes with limitations designed at reducing risks to the financial system, questions have been raised about the vulnerabilities it may create, according to a media report on Friday.

The decision has prompted concerns by banks and Maxine Waters, the top Democrat on the House Financial Services Committee, over potential risks to the financial system, Reuters reported.

Kraken (KRAKEN) is the first crypto firm to get a so-called “skinny” account, which is aimed to facilitate payments and doesn't come with the full complement of typical master account features, such as interest on deposits. The account was  approved by the Fed in March for an initial term of one year.

Those raising the concerns also contend that the Fed's approval process wasn't transparent and didn't follow Fed protocols, Reuters reported. Waters has asked the Kansas City Fed, which manages the account, to disclose more details by Friday.

The account allows Kraken's banking arm to access the central bank's wholesale payment system, Fedwire, and hold limited balances overnight, a company spokesperson told Reuters. But the company can't earn interest on reserve balances, as most other accountholders do, or access emergency Fed lending or its other payment systems — ACH and FedNow, the spokesperson said.

While Kraken's (KRAKEN) access to the payment system does present a threat to banks, in that it eliminates the need for crypto firms to use intermediaries for payments, some regulatory experts say the banks' concerns about risk are warranted.

Lenders have warned that allowing crypto companies access to Fedwire may create money-laundering and operational risks and could pull liquidity out of the banking system, Reuters said.

"The concern is by introducing institutions that may have less of a track record, less rigorous compliance and operations, even if they have limited models, that it could create a degree of systemic risk," ​Richard Levin, chair of the fintech practice at Taft ​Stettinius & Hollister, told Reuters.

In December, Fed Governor Michael Barr, who opposed the new payment account, saw increased exposure to money laundering as a risk. 

The Kraken spokesperson told Reuters that its bank reserves are fully backed and that the company complies with all bank-grade anti-money laundering and know-your-customer requirements. 

Last month, Fed Vice Chair for Supervision Michelle Bowman said the approval of the Kraken account doesn't necessarily imply an opening of the floodgates, acknowledging that the new account represents uncharted territory. "It's a bit of an experiment," she said.

data.title: Berkshire Hathaway sells yen-denominated bonds in first such deal after Abel took over - report
data.description:
Berkshire Hathaway (BRK.A) (BRK.B) has sold ¥272.3B (~$1.7B) worth of yen-denominated bonds, the first such deal since CEO Greg Abel took charge, Bloomberg News reported on Friday.

The offering comprised six tranches, with maturities ranging from three to thirty years, the report noted.

The deal, Berkshire's third-largest yen deal on record, comes amid rising volatility in Japanese government bonds as the Iran conflict drags on, according to the report.

The Omaha, Nebraska-based company has reportedly expanded its investment in the country recently, having bought stakes in major trading houses and Japanese insurance firm Tokio Marine Holdings.

The 10-year notes were said to be priced at a 90-basis-point spread over benchmarks, with a coupon of 3.084%. Comparatively, a previous November 2025 yen deal by Berkshire carried a coupon of 2.422%.

Furthermore, the notes were priced at a yield above those of other issuers, such as Crédit Agricole (CRARF) (CRARY), reflecting the bond market volatility.

Japan-related ETFs: (EWJ), (DXJ), (EWJV), (DFJ), (DBJP), (HEWJ), (FLJP), (OPPJ), (BBJP), (SCJ), (EZJ), (EWV)

data.title: CoreWeave prices upsized $3.5 billion convertible senior notes offering
data.description:
• CoreWeave (CRWV) on Friday said it priced an upsized $3.5 billion private offering of 1.75% convertible senior notes due 2032, increased from a previously announced $3.0 billion.
  • The company said the offering is expected to close on April 14 and includes an option for initial purchasers to buy up to an additional $500 million of notes.
  • CoreWeave expects net proceeds of about $3.45 billion, which it plans to use for general corporate purposes, with a portion allocated to capped call transactions.
  • The notes will pay 1.75% interest and be convertible into cash, shares or a combination of both, with an initial conversion price of about $119.60 per share, a roughly 30% premium to the stock’s last closing price.
  • CRWV +3.92% premarket to $95.61.
  • Source: Press Release
